intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant Beadlea glabrescens. However, I can provide information about a different plant, assuming you meant "Buddleja glabrescens" which is a species of flowering plant. 

Buddleja glabrescens is a plant species in the family Scrophulariaceae, characterized by its clusters of small, fragrant flowers and a shrub-like growth habit.

The plant is used in traditional medicine, and its extracts have shown potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. 

The bioactive constituents of Buddleja glabrescens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

Buddleja glabrescens is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
